name: Caldas de Reis - vuelta de Moliños
region_intro: Mountain biking around Caldas de Reis offers a diverse landscape characterized by green, hilly terrain shaped by the Umia River and its tributaries. The region features historical paths, ancient Roman bridges, and lush oak and eucalyptus forests, providing varied environments for cycling. Topography includes natural features like waterfalls and monumental rock formations, creating a dynamic backdrop for mountain bike trails. This blend of natural beauty and historical elements makes for engaging rides across different difficulty levels. Best mountain bike trails around Caldas De Reis The most popular mountain bike trail is Caldas de Reis - vuelta de Moliños, a 17.7 miles (28.4 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied terrain, passing by historical mills along the river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vía Verde del Salnes. – Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River loop from Caldas de Reis, a moderate 16.7 miles (27.0 km) path. This route leads through the Vía Verde (Greenway) and past a Roman bridge. Local mountain bikers also love the Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River – Umia River Trail loop from Caldas de Reis, a 24.4 miles (39.2 km) trail leading through river sections and varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Caldas de Reis is defined by its Umia River valleys, hilly terrain, and historical paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Caldas de Reis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Caldas de Reis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Caldas de Reis?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Caldas de Reis, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. These include 13 easy routes, 85 moderate routes, and 38 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Caldas de Reis?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, characterized by green, hilly terrain shaped by the Umia River and its tributaries. You'll encounter both challenging ascents and scenic descents, historical paths, ancient Roman bridges, and lush oak groves. Some routes also feature monumental rock formations and river sections.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls in Caldas de Reis?
A: Yes, several mountain bike routes in the area pass by beautiful waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40340991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azucarera de Portas – Barosa Waterfall loop from Estación de Portas&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to other cascades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valga Mills Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1536265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raxoi Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Caldas de Reis?
A: Yes, there are 13 easy mountain bike routes suitable for families. These trails typically feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20494840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Salnes. – Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a greenway, is a good option for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Caldas de Reis are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983424246/caldas-de-reis-vuelta-de-molinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis - vuelta de Moliños&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall – Azucarera de Portas loop from Estación de Portas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Caldas de Reis?
A: Caldas de Reis, being in Galicia, benefits from a generally mild climate. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures and lush green landscapes. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically mild but can be wet. Checking the local weather forecast before your ride is always recommended.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Caldas de Reis?
A: The mountain bike routes in Caldas de Reis are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic river sections, and the integration of historical elements like Roman bridges and old mills into the routes.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt;, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Hot Spring Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2663481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artistic Botanical Garden of Padrón&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4408534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Santiaguiño do Monte&lt;/a&gt;. The region's unique blend of natural beauty and historical sites makes for a rich riding experience.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Yes, Caldas de Reis offers 38 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. These trails typically feature significant elevation changes and more technical sections. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31920866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Segade Waterfall – Roman Bridge over the Bermaña River loop from Caldas de Reis&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbs and descents.

Q: Can I find parking near the mountain bike trails in Caldas de Reis?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Caldas de Reis, particularly near trailheads or in the town center. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information. Many routes, like those starting from Estación de Portas, have accessible parking.

Q: Are there any services like bike rental or repair shops in Caldas de Reis?
A: As a popular stop on the Camino de Santiago and a growing destination for active tourism, Caldas de Reis typically offers services for cyclists, including bike rental and repair shops. It's recommended to search locally for current options before your visit.
tips: Wooded trails along the Umia River. The route leads to a viewpoint of the Segade Waterfall. The waterfall cascades over 30m creating different pools in the granite rock
14 old water mills stone buildings containing circular grinding stones along the Barosa River. This trail is part of the Portuguese pilgrimage route to Santiago de Compostela
Old sugar factory with a tall red brick chimney on the outskirts of Caldas de Ries. Today the building is used as a nursery, day center and exhibition hall.
